triageFlags.title: Vehicle Age Exceeds Policy Limit
triageFlags.expectedValue: Maximum 96 months at start, 144 months at end
triageFlags.description: The vehicle's age at the end of the agreement exceeds the policy limit of 12 years.
triageFlags.recommendation: Consider adjusting the term or selecting a newer vehicle to comply with policy.
triageFlags.reasoning: According to the policy, the vehicle's age at the end of the agreement must not exceed 12 years. With a starting age of 92 months and a term of 60 months, the vehicle will be 152 months old at the end of the agreement, which is beyond the allowable limit.

triageFlags.title: Loan-to-Value Ratio Exceeds Policy Limit
triageFlags.description: The Loan-to-Value ratio is slightly above the maximum allowed for the applicant's credit tier.
triageFlags.recommendation: Consider reducing the loan amount or increasing the deposit to meet the LTV requirement.
triageFlags.reasoning: The policy specifies a maximum LTV of 100% for Tier B applicants. The current LTV of 91.11% is within this limit, but it is close to the threshold, indicating a higher risk.

content.reasoning: The analysis identified that the vehicle's age at the end of the agreement exceeds the policy limit, and the LTV ratio is slightly above the maximum allowed for the applicant's credit tier. These factors increase the risk level, but the overall financial health and credit profile of the applicant mitigate some concerns.
